predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the Federal Reserve will finalize (via Federal Register final rule or Fed press release) a policy change allowing banks/credit unions to access FedNow through approved intermediaries (i.e., indirect participation rather than only direct participants).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a Fed/Board final rule, operating circular update, or Federal Register final rule explicitly authorizes indirect/intermediated access to FedNow (not merely a proposal or request for comment).
evidence: Fed proposed allowing intermediaries for FedNow access (proposal stage). https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/other20260408a.htm
monitoring_signals: Federal Register docket movement from NPRM to final rule
FedNow Operating Procedures/Operating Circular updates
Fed press releases on payments/financial market infrastructures
predictions.rationale: The Fed has already publicly proposed the change, making a within-year finalization plausible, but implementation details and industry feedback could delay.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the FOMC will reduce the target range for the federal funds rate by at least 25 basis points versus the target range in effect on 2026-04-09.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if at any scheduled or unscheduled FOMC decision before the deadline the published target range’s upper bound is at least 0.25 percentage points lower than on 2026-04-09.
evidence: Fed minutes indicate officials still expect at least one 2026 cut (baseline disinflation/cooling demand). https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/monetary20260408a.htm
monitoring_signals: CPI/PCE inflation trend and labor-market cooling indicators
FOMC meeting statements and SEP/dot plot updates
Market-implied cuts (OIS/fed funds futures)
predictions.rationale: Minutes point to at least one cut as baseline; the main risk is re-acceleration in inflation or renewed energy shock that delays easing.

predictions.text: By 2026-06-30, Iran will publicly announce an official policy to levy a fee/toll on commercial shipping transiting the Strait of Hormuz (including either a schedule of charges or a stated mechanism for collection).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an Iranian government body (e.g., Ministry of Roads/Ports, IRGC-linked authority, cabinet spokesperson) issues a public statement/decree explicitly establishing a transit fee/toll for Hormuz passage for commercial vessels, with at least one implementable detail (rate, eligibility, collection method, or start date).
evidence: Reporting highlights Iran leverage via potential Hormuz tolls and U.S. demands for unrestricted passage. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/08/trump-iran-ceasefire-strait-of-hormuz-toll.html
monitoring_signals: Iranian cabinet/port authority announcements
UKMTO/CMF advisories referencing new Iranian transit requirements
Shipping/insurer circulars mentioning fees or required documentation
predictions.rationale: Tehran may keep the threat as leverage without formalizing it; implementing a toll invites legal/coalition pushback and enforcement challenges.

predictions.text: By 2026-07-31, the U.S. will impose (via presidential proclamation, USTR/Commerce notice, or Federal Register action) an additional 50% tariff on imports from at least one specific country explicitly justified as retaliation for supplying weapons to Iran.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if an official U.S. action sets an added 50% tariff rate (or 50 percentage-point surcharge) on imports from at least one named country, and the justification text explicitly cites supplying/providing weapons to Iran (or equivalent phrasing). Threats without enacted tariff action do not count.
evidence: Trump threatened 50% tariffs on countries supplying weapons to Iran. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/08/trump-threatens-tariffs-countries-supplying-weapons-iran-ceasefire.html
monitoring_signals: White House tariff proclamations and USTR statements
Federal Register tariff actions and CBP guidance updates
Diplomatic reporting on identified 'supplier' countries
predictions.rationale: Threats often aim to deter rather than execute; execution depends on attribution confidence and broader trade-war considerations.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, OpenAI will publicly file an IPO registration statement (S-1 or F-1) on SEC EDGAR for a U.S. listing (confidential submissions do not count).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if SEC EDGAR shows an S-1/F-1 filing by an OpenAI-branded issuer for an initial public offering (not a debt shelf, not a subsidiary with unrelated business). A press report without an EDGAR filing does not count.
evidence: Reporting says OpenAI plans an IPO with retail share allocation. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/08/openai-ipo-sarah-friar-retail-investors.html
monitoring_signals: EDGAR new issuer filings mentioning OpenAI
OpenAI official announcements about IPO timing/structure
Lead-underwriter rumors and S-1 drafting milestones in credible reporting
predictions.rationale: IPO intent is discussed, but governance, regulatory, and market-window constraints make a public filing within 2026 uncertain.

predictions.text: By 2026-09-30, the S&P 500 index will record a new all-time high closing level (strictly above its prior record close as of 2026-04-09).
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the official S&P 500 closing value on any trading day on/before the deadline is higher than the highest prior official close observed up to 2026-04-09.
evidence: Market commentary notes improved risk appetite after reclaiming key moving averages. https://www.marketwatch.com/story/the-s-p-500-smashed-back-above-2-key-moving-averages-in-a-rare-display-of-strength-heres-what-history-shows-happens-next-7141e5fe
monitoring_signals: S&P 500 breadth/earnings revision trends
Inflation/rates expectations (soft-landing vs re-acceleration)
Volatility regime (VIX) and credit spreads
predictions.rationale: Technical rebound and easing oil-shock fears help, but earnings downgrades or renewed conflict escalation could cap rallies.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, at least one U.S. state will enact (bill signed into law) a right-to-repair statute that explicitly covers agricultural equipment and requires OEMs to provide independent repair providers/owners with access to parts, tools, and/or repair documentation on fair terms.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a state-level law is enacted and its statutory text explicitly applies to agricultural/farm equipment and contains at least one access mandate (parts/tools/diagnostics/manuals) for owners/independent repairers.
evidence: John Deere’s $99M settlement signals rising pressure for right-to-repair access. https://www.thedrive.com/news/john-deere-to-pay-99-million-in-monumental-right-to-repair-settlement
monitoring_signals: State legislative trackers for right-to-repair bills (farm equipment scope)
Industry lobbying positions and OEM voluntary program announcements
AG/FTC actions that spur legislative momentum
predictions.rationale: The Deere settlement increases political salience; passage still depends on state calendars, committee dynamics, and OEM concessions.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, at least one major agricultural or construction equipment OEM other than John Deere (e.g., CNH Industrial, AGCO, Caterpillar, Komatsu) will announce a binding right-to-repair agreement/settlement with a U.S. state AG (or a multistate coalition) that includes providing diagnostic software/tools to equipment owners or independent repair shops.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if a state AG office (or coalition) publishes a settlement/assurance-of-voluntary-compliance (or equivalent binding agreement), and the agreement explicitly commits the OEM to provide diagnostics/tools/software access beyond authorized dealers.
evidence: Deere settlement increases sector-wide pressure and creates a template. https://www.thedrive.com/news/john-deere-to-pay-99-million-in-monumental-right-to-repair-settlement
monitoring_signals: State AG press releases and settlement databases
OEM policy updates on diagnostics/subscription tools
Repair advocacy group announcements (PIRG, Farm Action, etc.)
predictions.rationale: Once one OEM settles, others face copycat enforcement risk; however, firms may try voluntary programs to avoid binding settlements.

predictions.text: By 2026-12-31, the U.S. 10-year Treasury constant maturity rate (FRED series DGS10) will print at or below 3.50% on at least one trading day.
predictions.verification_criteria: Resolves YES if the daily DGS10 value published by the St. Louis Fed is ≤ 3.50 for any date on/before 2026-12-31.
evidence: Commentary suggests bonds may benefit as oil-shock fears ease and rate-cut expectations return. https://www.marketwatch.com/story/bonds-may-be-the-real-winner-now-that-the-world-economy-has-sidestepped-a-historic-oil-crisis-4797c6ff
Fed minutes still expect at least one 2026 cut. https://www.federalreserve.gov/newsevents/pressreleases/monetary20260408a.htm
monitoring_signals: Core inflation trajectory and recession probability indicators
Term premium estimates and Treasury refunding/issuance surprises
Risk-off episodes tied to geopolitics or credit stress
predictions.rationale: A move to 3.5% likely requires clear disinflation and/or risk-off growth fears; supply/term-premium pressures are the main counterweight.
